be-read: Read file content with line numbers. Returns viewed_code_id; pass it to be-replace to verify the target range has not changed.
be-replace: Replace a precise line range in a file. Pass viewed_code_id from a prior be-read to validate consistency; if old is provided it must match the current content exactly.
be-replace-old: Optional. If provided, must match the current file content exactly; otherwise the tool returns an error.
be-replace-viewed-code-id: Obtained from the most recent be-read result, used to detect whether the target range has changed.
be-insert: Insert content after a specific line.
be-write: Write raw content to a file, with a degraded parser for malformed JSON payloads.
be-func-range: Find the enclosing function or brace block for a given line.
be-tag-range: Find the enclosing XML/HTML/Vue tag pair for a given line.
be-balance: Check bracket, brace, parenthesis, tag, and quote balance.
be-insert-chip: Insert content from a file (file://) or chip cache (chip://). Omit from to list all chip IDs.
be-trx: Transaction management: commit, rollback, or inspect pending edit snapshots.
be-trx-action: Transaction action: commit, rollback, or status.
be-trx-step: Only used for rollback. Number of recent snapshots to roll back; defaults to 1.
